{{Short description|Kofun period keyhole-shaped burial mound in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tsukawara Kofun Cluster | native_name =塚原古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Kumamoto Tsukawara Ancient Tomb Park 07.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' at Tsukawara Kofun Cluster | map_type = Japan Kumamoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tsukawara Kofun Clsuter | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|32|41|23|N|130|44|13|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Minami-ku, Kumamoto]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th-6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park, museum) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|41|23|N|130|44|13|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Tsukawara Kofun Cluster'''|塚原古墳群|Tsukawara Kofun gun}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun| burial mound]]s, located in the Jonan neighborhood of [[Minami-ku, Kumamoto|Minami-ku]] in the city of [[Kumamoto, Kumamoto|Kumamoto]], [[Japan]]. The tumulus cluster was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/164081|title=塚原古墳群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tsukawara Kofun cluster was discovered in 1972 during the construction of the [[Kyushu Expressway]], and for preservation purposes, the route of the expressway was changed to a tunnel which passes directly underneath the site. The site was preserved as Tsukahara Kofun Park, and also contains the Kumamoto City Tsukahara History and Folklore Museum. This group of burial mounds is the largest in Kumamoto Prefecture and one of the largest in Japan, and was constructed in the 4th to 6th centuries. The park contains 39 {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳}} square moated tumuli, 34 {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳}} circular tumuli, and three {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}} keyhole-shaped tombs, with one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, along with 18 stone [[sarcophagus|sarcophagi]], and one stone-covered earthen mound; however, only the area immediately above the route of the Kyushu Expressway has been investigated and preserved. Of the two hills in the park, north and south, the large square moat tombs are mainly concentrated south of the northern hill, while the large circular tombs are concentrated around the northern hill and south of the southern hill. It is believed that there are many more such burial mounds outside the park, and in an exploratory survey of the entire Tsukahara Plateau in 1973, and additional 203 burial mounds were confirmed, and it was estimated that in the surrounding area there may be nearly 500 burial mounds in total. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
<gallery> Kumamoto Tsukawara Ancient Tomb Park 08.JPG| ''enpun''-style round kofun Kumamoto Tsukawara Ancient Tomb Park 04.JPG| ''hōfun''-style square kofun Kumamoto Tsukawara Ancient Tomb Park 01.JPG| Reconstructed building at Tsukawara Historical Museum Kumamoto City Tsukawara Historical Museum.JPG|Kumamoto City Tsukawara Historical Museum </gallery>
The site is about 30 minutes on car from [[Uto Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Kagoshima Main Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:-inline|Tsukawara Ancient Tomb Park}} *[https://kumamoto-guide.jp/spots/detail/193 Kumamoto Heart-to-Heart Sightseeing guide]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://tsukawara.kumamoto-city-museum.jp|Kumamoto City Tsukahara History and Folklore Museum]{{in lang|ja}}

{{Short description|Kofun period keyhole-shaped burial mound in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tsukawara Kofun Cluster | native_name =塚原古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Kumamoto Tsukawara Ancient Tomb Park 07.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' at Tsukawara Kofun Cluster | map_type = Japan Kumamoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tsukawara Kofun Clsuter | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|32|41|23|N|130|44|13|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Minami-ku, Kumamoto]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th-6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park, museum) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|41|23|N|130|44|13|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Tsukawara Kofun Cluster'''|塚原古墳群|Tsukawara Kofun gun}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun| burial mound]]s, located in the Jonan neighborhood of [[Minami-ku, Kumamoto|Minami-ku]] in the city of [[Kumamoto, Kumamoto|Kumamoto]], [[Japan]]. The tumulus cluster was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/164081|title=塚原古墳群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tsukawara Kofun cluster was discovered in 1972 during the construction of the [[Kyushu Expressway]], and for preservation purposes, the route of the expressway was changed to a tunnel which passes directly underneath the site. The site was preserved as Tsukahara Kofun Park, and also contains the Kumamoto City Tsukahara History and Folklore Museum. This group of burial mounds is the largest in Kumamoto Prefecture and one of the largest in Japan, and was constructed in the 4th to 6th centuries. The park contains 39 {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳}} square moated tumuli, 34 {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} circular tumuli, and three {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}} keyhole-shaped tombs, with one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, along with 18 stone [[sarcophagus|sarcophagi]], and one stone-covered earthen mound; however, only the area immediately above the route of the Kyushu Expressway has been investigated and preserved. Of the two hills in the park, north and south, the large square moat tombs are mainly concentrated south of the northern hill, while the large circular tombs are concentrated around the northern hill and south of the southern hill. It is believed that there are many more such burial mounds outside the park, and in an exploratory survey of the entire Tsukahara Plateau in 1973, and additional 203 burial mounds were confirmed, and it was estimated that in the surrounding area there may be nearly 500 burial mounds in total. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
<gallery> Kumamoto Tsukawara Ancient Tomb Park 08.JPG| ''enpun''-style round kofun Kumamoto Tsukawara Ancient Tomb Park 04.JPG| ''hōfun''-style square kofun Kumamoto Tsukawara Ancient Tomb Park 01.JPG| Reconstructed building at Tsukawara Historical Museum Kumamoto City Tsukawara Historical Museum.JPG|Kumamoto City Tsukawara Historical Museum </gallery>
The site is about 30 minutes on car from [[Uto Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Kagoshima Main Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)]]

{{nihongo|'''Tsukawara Kofun Cluster'''|塚原古墳群|Tsukawara Kofun gun}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun| burial mound]]s, located in the Jonan neighborhood of [[Minami-ku, Kumamoto|Minami-ku]] in the city of [[Kumamoto, Kumamoto|Kumamoto]], [[Japan]]. The tumulus cluster was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/164081|title=塚原古墳群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tsukawara Kofun cluster was discovered in 1972 during the construction of the [[Kyushu Expressway]], and for preservation purposes, the route of the expressway was changed to a tunnel which passes directly underneath the site. The site was preserved as Tsukahara Kofun Park, and also contains the Kumamoto City Tsukahara History and Folklore Museum. This group of burial mounds is the largest in Kumamoto Prefecture and one of the largest in Japan, and was constructed in the 4th to 6th centuries. The park contains 39 {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} square moated tumuli, 34 {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} circular tumuli, and three {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}} keyhole-shaped tombs, with one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, along with 18 stone [[sarcophagus|sarcophagi]], and one stone-covered earthen mound; however, only the area immediately above the route of the Kyushu Expressway has been investigated and preserved. Of the two hills in the park, north and south, the large square moat tombs are mainly concentrated south of the northern hill, while the large circular tombs are concentrated around the northern hill and south of the southern hill. It is believed that there are many more such burial mounds outside the park, and in an exploratory survey of the entire Tsukahara Plateau in 1973, and additional 203 burial mounds were confirmed, and it was estimated that in the surrounding area there may be nearly 500 burial mounds in total. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
<gallery> Kumamoto Tsukawara Ancient Tomb Park 08.JPG| ''enpun''-style round kofun Kumamoto Tsukawara Ancient Tomb Park 04.JPG| ''hōfun''-style square kofun Kumamoto Tsukawara Ancient Tomb Park 01.JPG| Reconstructed building at Tsukawara Historical Museum Kumamoto City Tsukawara Historical Museum.JPG|Kumamoto City Tsukawara Historical Museum </gallery>
The site is about 30 minutes on car from [[Uto Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Kagoshima Main Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:-inline|Tsukawara Ancient Tomb Park}} *[https://kumamoto-guide.jp/spots/detail/193 Kumamoto Heart-to-Heart Sightseeing guide]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://tsukawara.kumamoto-city-museum.jp|Kumamoto City Tsukahara History and Folklore Museum]{{in lang|ja}}

Of the two hills in the park, north and south, the large square moat tombs are mainly concentrated south of the northern hill, while the large circular tombs are concentrated around the northern hill and south of the southern hill. It is believed that there are many more such burial mounds outside the park, and in an exploratory survey of the entire Tsukahara Plateau in 1973, and additional 203 burial mounds were confirmed, and it was estimated that in the surrounding area there may be nearly 500 burial mounds in total. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

{{Short description|Kofun period keyhole-shaped Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tsukawara Kofun Cluster | native_name =塚原古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Kumamoto Tsukawara Ancient Tomb Park 07.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' at Tsukawara Kofun Cluster | map_type = Japan Kumamoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tsukawara Kofun Clsuter | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|32|41|23|N|130|44|13|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Minami-ku, Kumamoto]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th-6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park, museum) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|41|23|N|130|44|13|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Tsukawara Kofun Cluster'''|塚原古墳群|Tsukawara Kofun gun}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun| Kofun]]s, located in the Jonan neighborhood of [[Minami-ku, Kumamoto|Minami-ku]] in the city of [[Kumamoto, Kumamoto|Kumamoto]], [[Japan]]. The tumulus cluster was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/164081|title=塚原古墳群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tsukawara Kofun cluster was discovered in 1972 during the construction of the [[Kyushu Expressway]], and for preservation purposes, the route of the expressway was changed to a tunnel which passes directly underneath the site. The site was preserved as Tsukahara Kofun Park, and also contains the Kumamoto City Tsukahara History and Folklore Museum. This group of Kofuns is the largest in Kumamoto Prefecture and one of the largest in Japan, and was constructed in the 4th to 6th centuries. The park contains 39 {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} square moated tumuli, 34 {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} circular tumuli, and three {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} keyhole-shaped tombs, with one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, along with 18 stone [[sarcophagus|sarcophagi]], and one stone-covered earthen mound; however, only the area immediately above the route of the Kyushu Expressway has been investigated and preserved. Of the two hills in the park, north and south, the large square moat tombs are mainly concentrated south of the northern hill, while the large circular tombs are concentrated around the northern hill and south of the southern hill. It is believed that there are many more such Kofuns outside the park, and in an exploratory survey of the entire Tsukahara Plateau in 1973, and additional 203 Kofuns were confirmed, and it was estimated that in the surrounding area there may be nearly 500 Kofuns in total. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
<gallery> Kumamoto Tsukawara Ancient Tomb Park 08.JPG| ''enpun''-style round kofun Kumamoto Tsukawara Ancient Tomb Park 04.JPG| ''hōfun''-style square kofun Kumamoto Tsukawara Ancient Tomb Park 01.JPG| Reconstructed building at Tsukawara Historical Museum Kumamoto City Tsukawara Historical Museum.JPG|Kumamoto City Tsukawara Historical Museum </gallery>
The site is about 30 minutes on car from [[Uto Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Kagoshima Main Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)]]

The park contains 39 {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} square moated tumuli, 34 {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} circular tumuli, and three {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} keyhole-shaped tombs, with one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, along with 18 stone [[sarcophagus|sarcophagi]], and one stone-covered earthen mound; however, only the area immediately above the route of the Kyushu Expressway has been investigated and preserved. Of the two hills in the park, north and south, the large square moat tombs are mainly concentrated south of the northern hill, while the large circular tombs are concentrated around the northern hill and south of the southern hill. It is believed that there are many more such Kofuns outside the park, and in an exploratory survey of the entire Tsukahara Plateau in 1973, and additional 203 Kofuns were confirmed, and it was estimated that in the surrounding area there may be nearly 500 Kofuns in total. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
<gallery> Kumamoto Tsukawara Ancient Tomb Park 08.JPG| ''enpun''-style round kofun Kumamoto Tsukawara Ancient Tomb Park 04.JPG| ''hōfun''-style square kofun Kumamoto Tsukawara Ancient Tomb Park 01.JPG| Reconstructed building at Tsukawara Historical Museum Kumamoto City Tsukawara Historical Museum.JPG|Kumamoto City Tsukawara Historical Museum </gallery>
The site is about 30 minutes on car from [[Uto Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Kagoshima Main Line]].

{{Short description|Kofun period keyhole-shaped Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tsukawara Kofun Cluster | native_name =塚原古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Kumamoto Tsukawara Ancient Tomb Park 07.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' at Tsukawara Kofun Cluster | map_type = Japan Kumamoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tsukawara Kofun Clsuter | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|32|41|23|N|130|44|13|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Minami-ku, Kumamoto]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th-6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park, museum) | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|41|23|N|130|44|13|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Tsukawara Kofun Cluster'''|塚原古墳群|Tsukawara Kofun gun}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun| Kofun]]s, located in the Jonan neighborhood of [[Minami-ku, Kumamoto|Minami-ku]] in the city of [[Kumamoto, Kumamoto|Kumamoto]], [[Japan]]. The tumulus cluster was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/164081|title=塚原古墳群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tsukawara Kofun cluster was discovered in 1972 during the construction of the [[Kyushu Expressway]], and for preservation purposes, the route of the expressway was changed to a tunnel which passes directly underneath the site. The site was preserved as Tsukahara Kofun Park, and also contains the Kumamoto City Tsukahara History and Folklore Museum. This group of Kofuns is the largest in Kumamoto Prefecture and one of the largest in Japan, and was constructed in the 4th to 6th centuries. The park contains 39 {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} square moated tumuli, 34 {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} circular tumuli, and three {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} keyhole-shaped tombs, with one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, along with 18 stone [[sarcophagus|sarcophagi]], and one stone-covered earthen mound; however, only the area immediately above the route of the Kyushu Expressway has been investigated and preserved. Of the two hills in the park, north and south, the large square moat tombs are mainly concentrated south of the northern hill, while the large circular tombs are concentrated around the northern hill and south of the southern hill. It is believed that there are many more such Kofuns outside the park, and in an exploratory survey of the entire Tsukahara Plateau in 1973, and additional 203 Kofuns were confirmed, and it was estimated that in the surrounding area there may be nearly 500 Kofuns in total. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
<gallery> Kumamoto Tsukawara Ancient Tomb Park 08.JPG| ''enpun''-style round kofun Kumamoto Tsukawara Ancient Tomb Park 04.JPG| ''hōfun''-style square kofun Kumamoto Tsukawara Ancient Tomb Park 01.JPG| Reconstructed building at Tsukawara Historical Museum Kumamoto City Tsukawara Historical Museum.JPG|Kumamoto City Tsukawara Historical Museum </gallery>
The site is about 30 minutes on car from [[Uto Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Kagoshima Main Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:-inline|Tsukawara Ancient Tomb Park}} *[https://kumamoto-guide.jp/spots/detail/193 Kumamoto Heart-to-Heart Sightseeing guide]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://tsukawara.kumamoto-city-museum.jp|Kumamoto City Tsukahara History and Folklore Museum]{{in lang|ja}}

revision.text:
{{Short description|Kofun period keyhole-shaped Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tsukawara Kofun Cluster | native_name =塚原古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Kumamoto Tsukawara Ancient Tomb Park 07.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' at Tsukawara Kofun Cluster | map_type = Japan Kumamoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tsukawara Kofun Clsuter | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|32|41|23|N|130|44|13|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Minami-ku, Kumamoto]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th-6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park, museum) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|41|23|N|130|44|13|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Tsukawara Kofun Cluster'''|塚原古墳群|Tsukawara Kofun gun}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun| Kofun]]s, located in the Jonan neighborhood of [[Minami-ku, Kumamoto|Minami-ku]] in the city of [[:en:Kumamoto, Kumamoto|Kumamoto]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus cluster was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/164081|title=塚原古墳群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tsukawara Kofun cluster was discovered in 1972 during the construction of the [[:en:Kyushu Expressway|Kyushu Expressway]], and for preservation purposes, the route of the expressway was changed to a tunnel which passes directly underneath the site. The site was preserved as Tsukahara Kofun Park, and also contains the Kumamoto City Tsukahara History and Folklore Museum. This group of Kofuns is the largest in Kumamoto Prefecture and one of the largest in Japan, and was constructed in the 4th to 6th centuries. The park contains 39 {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} square moated tumuli, 34 {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} circular tumuli, and three {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}} keyhole-shaped tombs, with one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, along with 18 stone [[sarcophagus|sarcophagi]], and one stone-covered earthen mound; however, only the area immediately above the route of the Kyushu Expressway has been investigated and preserved. Of the two hills in the park, north and south, the large square moat tombs are mainly concentrated south of the northern hill, while the large circular tombs are concentrated around the northern hill and south of the southern hill. It is believed that there are many more such Kofuns outside the park, and in an exploratory survey of the entire Tsukahara Plateau in 1973, and additional 203 Kofuns were confirmed, and it was estimated that in the surrounding area there may be nearly 500 Kofuns in total. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
<gallery> Kumamoto Tsukawara Ancient Tomb Park 08.JPG| ''enpun''-style round kofun Kumamoto Tsukawara Ancient Tomb Park 04.JPG| ''hōfun''-style square kofun Kumamoto Tsukawara Ancient Tomb Park 01.JPG| Reconstructed building at Tsukawara Historical Museum Kumamoto City Tsukawara Historical Museum.JPG|Kumamoto City Tsukawara Historical Museum </gallery>
The site is about 30 minutes on car from [[Uto Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Kagoshima Main Line|Kagoshima Main Line]].
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:-inline|Tsukawara Ancient Tomb Park}} *[https://kumamoto-guide.jp/spots/detail/193 Kumamoto Heart-to-Heart Sightseeing guide]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://tsukawara.kumamoto-city-museum.jp|Kumamoto City Tsukahara History and Folklore Museum]{{in lang|ja}}

{{nihongo|'''Tsukawara Kofun Cluster'''|塚原古墳群|Tsukawara Kofun gun}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun| Kofun]]s, located in the Jonan neighborhood of [[:en:Minami-ku, Kumamoto|Minami-ku]] in the city of [[:en:Kumamoto, Kumamoto|Kumamoto]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus cluster was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/164081|title=塚原古墳群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tsukawara Kofun cluster was discovered in 1972 during the construction of the [[:en:Kyushu Expressway|Kyushu Expressway]], and for preservation purposes, the route of the expressway was changed to a tunnel which passes directly underneath the site. The site was preserved as Tsukahara Kofun Park, and also contains the Kumamoto City Tsukahara History and Folklore Museum. This group of Kofuns is the largest in Kumamoto Prefecture and one of the largest in Japan, and was constructed in the 4th to 6th centuries. The park contains 39 {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} square moated tumuli, 34 {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} circular tumuli, and three {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}} keyhole-shaped tombs, with one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, along with 18 stone [[:en:Sarcophagus|sarcophagi]], and one stone-covered earthen mound; however, only the area immediately above the route of the Kyushu Expressway has been investigated and preserved. Of the two hills in the park, north and south, the large square moat tombs are mainly concentrated south of the northern hill, while the large circular tombs are concentrated around the northern hill and south of the southern hill. It is believed that there are many more such Kofuns outside the park, and in an exploratory survey of the entire Tsukahara Plateau in 1973, and additional 203 Kofuns were confirmed, and it was estimated that in the surrounding area there may be nearly 500 Kofuns in total. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

The site is about 30 minutes on car from [[:en:Uto Station|Uto Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Kagoshima Main Line|Kagoshima Main Line]].

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tsukawara Ancient Tomb Park]][[en:Tsukawara Kofun Cluster]][[ja:塚原古墳群 (熊本市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q55532800]] Wikidata name: Tsukawara Kofun Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:06, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun period keyhole-shaped Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tsukawara Kofun Cluster | native_name =塚原古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image =Kumamoto Tsukawara Ancient Tomb Park 07.JPG|image= File:Kumamoto Tsukawara Ancient Tomb Park 07.JPG| image_size = | alt = | caption = ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' at Tsukawara Kofun Cluster | map_type = Japan Kumamoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tsukawara Kofun Clsuter | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|32|41|23|N|130|44|13|E|display=inline, title}}; {{Coord|32.689722222222215|N|130.73694444444442|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location =[[:en:Minami-ku, Kumamoto|Minami-ku, Kumamoto]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]; {{ill|Minami-ku|qid=Q876960}}| region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th-6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park, museum) | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|qid=Q30834580}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|41|23|N|130|44|13|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Tsukawara Kofun Cluster'''|塚原古墳群|Tsukawara Kofun gun}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun| Kofun]]s, located in the Jonan neighborhood of [[:en:Minami-ku, Kumamoto|Minami-ku]] in the city of [[:en:Kumamoto, Kumamoto|Kumamoto]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus cluster was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/164081|title=塚原古墳群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tsukawara Kofun cluster was discovered in 1972 during the construction of the [[:en:Kyushu Expressway|Kyushu Expressway]], and for preservation purposes, the route of the expressway was changed to a tunnel which passes directly underneath the site. The site was preserved as Tsukahara Kofun Park, and also contains the Kumamoto City Tsukahara History and Folklore Museum. This group of Kofuns is the largest in Kumamoto Prefecture and one of the largest in Japan, and was constructed in the 4th to 6th centuries. The park contains 39 {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} square moated tumuli, 34 {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} circular tumuli, and three {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}} keyhole-shaped tombs, with one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, along with 18 stone [[:en:Sarcophagus|sarcophagi]], and one stone-covered earthen mound; however, only the area immediately above the route of the Kyushu Expressway has been investigated and preserved. Of the two hills in the park, north and south, the large square moat tombs are mainly concentrated south of the northern hill, while the large circular tombs are concentrated around the northern hill and south of the southern hill. It is believed that there are many more such Kofuns outside the park, and in an exploratory survey of the entire Tsukahara Plateau in 1973, and additional 203 Kofuns were confirmed, and it was estimated that in the surrounding area there may be nearly 500 Kofuns in total. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
<gallery> Kumamoto Tsukawara Ancient Tomb Park 08.JPG| ''enpun''-style round kofun Kumamoto Tsukawara Ancient Tomb Park 04.JPG| ''hōfun''-style square kofun Kumamoto Tsukawara Ancient Tomb Park 01.JPG| Reconstructed building at Tsukawara Historical Museum Kumamoto City Tsukawara Historical Museum.JPG|Kumamoto City Tsukawara Historical Museum </gallery>
The site is about 30 minutes on car from [[:en:Uto Station|Uto Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Kagoshima Main Line|Kagoshima Main Line]].
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tsukawara Ancient Tomb Park]][[en:Tsukawara Kofun Cluster]][[ja:塚原古墳群 (熊本市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q55532800]] Wikidata name: Tsukawara Kofun Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:06, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun period keyhole-shaped Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tsukawara Kofun Cluster | native_name =塚原古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image =Kumamoto Tsukawara Ancient Tomb Park 07.JPG|image= File:Kumamoto Tsukawara Ancient Tomb Park 07.JPG| image_size = | alt = | caption = ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' at Tsukawara Kofun Cluster | map_type = Japan Kumamoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tsukawara Kofun Clsuter | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|32|41|23|N|130|44|13|E|display=inline, title}}; {{Coord|32.689722222222215|N|130.73694444444442|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location =[[:en:Minami-ku, Kumamoto|Minami-ku, Kumamoto]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]; {{ill|Minami-ku|WD=Q876960}}| region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th-6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park, museum) | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|41|23|N|130|44|13|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Tsukawara Kofun Cluster'''|塚原古墳群|Tsukawara Kofun gun}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun| Kofun]]s, located in the Jonan neighborhood of [[:en:Minami-ku, Kumamoto|Minami-ku]] in the city of [[:en:Kumamoto, Kumamoto|Kumamoto]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus cluster was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/164081|title=塚原古墳群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tsukawara Kofun cluster was discovered in 1972 during the construction of the [[:en:Kyushu Expressway|Kyushu Expressway]], and for preservation purposes, the route of the expressway was changed to a tunnel which passes directly underneath the site. The site was preserved as Tsukahara Kofun Park, and also contains the Kumamoto City Tsukahara History and Folklore Museum. This group of Kofuns is the largest in Kumamoto Prefecture and one of the largest in Japan, and was constructed in the 4th to 6th centuries. The park contains 39 {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} square moated tumuli, 34 {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} circular tumuli, and three {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}} keyhole-shaped tombs, with one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, along with 18 stone [[:en:Sarcophagus|sarcophagi]], and one stone-covered earthen mound; however, only the area immediately above the route of the Kyushu Expressway has been investigated and preserved. Of the two hills in the park, north and south, the large square moat tombs are mainly concentrated south of the northern hill, while the large circular tombs are concentrated around the northern hill and south of the southern hill. It is believed that there are many more such Kofuns outside the park, and in an exploratory survey of the entire Tsukahara Plateau in 1973, and additional 203 Kofuns were confirmed, and it was estimated that in the surrounding area there may be nearly 500 Kofuns in total. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
<gallery> Kumamoto Tsukawara Ancient Tomb Park 08.JPG| ''enpun''-style round kofun Kumamoto Tsukawara Ancient Tomb Park 04.JPG| ''hōfun''-style square kofun Kumamoto Tsukawara Ancient Tomb Park 01.JPG| Reconstructed building at Tsukawara Historical Museum Kumamoto City Tsukawara Historical Museum.JPG|Kumamoto City Tsukawara Historical Museum </gallery>
The site is about 30 minutes on car from [[:en:Uto Station|Uto Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Kagoshima Main Line|Kagoshima Main Line]].
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tsukawara Ancient Tomb Park]][[en:Tsukawara Kofun Cluster]][[ja:塚原古墳群 (熊本市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q55532800]] Wikidata name: Tsukawara Kofun Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:06, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun period keyhole-shaped Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tsukawara Kofun Cluster | native_name =塚原古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image =Kumamoto Tsukawara Ancient Tomb Park 07.JPG|image= File:Kumamoto Tsukawara Ancient Tomb Park 07.JPG| image_size = | alt = | caption = ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' at Tsukawara Kofun Cluster | map_type = Japan Kumamoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tsukawara Kofun Clsuter | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|32|41|23|N|130|44|13|E|display=inline, title}}; {{Coord|32.689722222222215|N|130.73694444444442|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location =[[:en:Minami-ku, Kumamoto|Minami-ku, Kumamoto]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]; {{ill|Minami-ku|WD=Q876960}}| region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th-6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park, museum) | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|41|23|N|130|44|13|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Tsukawara Kofun Cluster'''|塚原古墳群|Tsukawara Kofun gun}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun| Kofun]]s, located in the Jonan neighborhood of [[:en:Minami-ku, Kumamoto|Minami-ku]] in the city of [[:en:Kumamoto, Kumamoto|Kumamoto]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus cluster was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/164081|title=塚原古墳群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tsukawara Kofun cluster was discovered in 1972 during the construction of the [[:en:Kyushu Expressway|Kyushu Expressway]], and for preservation purposes, the route of the expressway was changed to a tunnel which passes directly underneath the site. The site was preserved as Tsukahara Kofun Park, and also contains the Kumamoto City Tsukahara History and Folklore Museum. This group of Kofuns is the largest in Kumamoto Prefecture and one of the largest in Japan, and was constructed in the 4th to 6th centuries. The park contains 39 {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} square moated tumuli, 34 {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} circular tumuli, and three {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}} keyhole-shaped tombs, with one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, along with 18 stone [[:en:Sarcophagus|sarcophagi]], and one stone-covered earthen mound; however, only the area immediately above the route of the Kyushu Expressway has been investigated and preserved. Of the two hills in the park, north and south, the large square moat tombs are mainly concentrated south of the northern hill, while the large circular tombs are concentrated around the northern hill and south of the southern hill. It is believed that there are many more such Kofuns outside the park, and in an exploratory survey of the entire Tsukahara Plateau in 1973, and additional 203 Kofuns were confirmed, and it was estimated that in the surrounding area there may be nearly 500 Kofuns in total. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
<gallery> Kumamoto Tsukawara Ancient Tomb Park 08.JPG| ''enpun''-style round kofun Kumamoto Tsukawara Ancient Tomb Park 04.JPG| ''hōfun''-style square kofun Kumamoto Tsukawara Ancient Tomb Park 01.JPG| Reconstructed building at Tsukawara Historical Museum Kumamoto City Tsukawara Historical Museum.JPG|Kumamoto City Tsukawara Historical Museum </gallery>
The site is about 30 minutes on car from [[:en:Uto Station|Uto Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Kagoshima Main Line|Kagoshima Main Line]].
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:-inline|Tsukawara Ancient Tomb Park}} *[https://kumamoto-guide.jp/spots/detail/193 Kumamoto Heart-to-Heart Sightseeing guide]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://tsukawara.kumamoto-city-museum.jp|Kumamoto City Tsukahara History and Folklore Museum]{{in lang|ja}}

{{nihongo|'''Tsukawara Kofun Cluster'''|塚原古墳群|Tsukawara Kofun gun}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun| Kofun]]s, located in the Jonan neighborhood of [[:en:Minami-ku, Kumamoto|Minami-ku]] in the city of [[:en:Kumamoto, Kumamoto|Kumamoto]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus cluster was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/164081|title=塚原古墳群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tsukawara Kofun cluster was discovered in 1972 during the construction of the [[:en:Kyushu Expressway|Kyushu Expressway]], and for preservation purposes, the route of the expressway was changed to a tunnel which passes directly underneath the site. The site was preserved as Tsukahara Kofun Park, and also contains the Kumamoto City Tsukahara History and Folklore Museum. This group of Kofuns is the largest in Kumamoto Prefecture and one of the largest in Japan, and was constructed in the 4th to 6th centuries. The park contains 39 {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} square moated tumuli, 34 {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} circular tumuli, and three {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}} keyhole-shaped tombs, with one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, along with 18 stone [[:en:Sarcophagus|sarcophagi]], and one stone-covered earthen mound; however, only the area immediately above the route of the Kyushu Expressway has been investigated and preserved. Of the two hills in the park, north and south, the large square moat tombs are mainly concentrated south of the northern hill, while the large circular tombs are concentrated around the northern hill and south of the southern hill. It is believed that there are many more such Kofuns outside the park, and in an exploratory survey of the entire Tsukahara Plateau in 1973, and additional 203 Kofuns were confirmed, and it was estimated that in the surrounding area there may be nearly 500 Kofuns in total. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

The site is about 30 minutes on car from [[:en:Uto Station|Uto Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Kagoshima Main Line|Kagoshima Main Line]].

The site is about 30 minutes on car from [[:en:Uto Station|Uto Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Kagoshima Main Line|Kagoshima Main Line]].

The park contains 39 {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} square moated tumuli, 34 {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} circular tumuli, and three {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}} keyhole-shaped tombs, with one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, along with 18 stone [[:en:Sarcophagus|sarcophagi]], and one stone-covered earthen mound; however, only the area immediately above the route of the Kyushu Expressway has been investigated and preserved. Of the two hills in the park, north and south, the large square moat tombs are mainly concentrated south of the northern hill, while the large circular tombs are concentrated around the northern hill and south of the southern hill. It is believed that there are many more such Kofuns outside the park, and in an exploratory survey of the entire Tsukahara Plateau in 1973, and additional 203 Kofuns were confirmed, and it was estimated that in the surrounding area there may be nearly 500 Kofuns in total. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

The site is about 30 minutes on car from [[:en:Uto Station|Uto Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Kagoshima Main Line|Kagoshima Main Line]].

The tumulus cluster was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/164081|title=塚原古墳群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tsukawara Kofun cluster was discovered in 1972 during the construction of the [[:en:Kyushu Expressway|Kyushu Expressway]], and for preservation purposes, the route of the expressway was changed to a tunnel which passes directly underneath the site. The site was preserved as Tsukahara Kofun Park, and also contains the Kumamoto City Tsukahara History and Folklore Museum. This group of Kofuns is the largest in Kumamoto Prefecture and one of the largest in Japan, and was constructed in the 4th to 6th centuries. The park contains 39 {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} square moated tumuli, 34 {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} circular tumuli, and three {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}} keyhole-shaped tombs, with one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, along with 18 stone [[:en:Sarcophagus|sarcophagi]], and one stone-covered earthen mound; however, only the area immediately above the route of the Kyushu Expressway has been investigated and preserved. Of the two hills in the park, north and south, the large square moat tombs are mainly concentrated south of the northern hill, while the large circular tombs are concentrated around the northern hill and south of the southern hill. It is believed that there are many more such Kofuns outside the park, and in an exploratory survey of the entire Tsukahara Plateau in 1973, and additional 203 Kofuns were confirmed, and it was estimated that in the surrounding area there may be nearly 500 Kofuns in total. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
<gallery> Kumamoto Tsukawara Ancient Tomb Park 08.JPG| ''enpun''-style round kofun Kumamoto Tsukawara Ancient Tomb Park 04.JPG| ''hōfun''-style square kofun Kumamoto Tsukawara Ancient Tomb Park 01.JPG| Reconstructed building at Tsukawara Historical Museum Kumamoto City Tsukawara Historical Museum.JPG|Kumamoto City Tsukawara Historical Museum </gallery>
The site is about 30 minutes on car from [[:en:Uto Station|Uto Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Kagoshima Main Line|Kagoshima Main Line]].
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:-inline|Tsukawara Ancient Tomb Park}} *[https://kumamoto-guide.jp/spots/detail/193 Kumamoto Heart-to-Heart Sightseeing guide]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://tsukawara.kumamoto-city-museum.jp|Kumamoto City Tsukahara History and Folklore Museum]{{in lang|ja}}

{{Coord|32.689722222222215|N|130.73694444444442|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location ={{ill|Minami-ku, Kumamoto|en|Minami-ku, Kumamoto|WD=Q876960|lt=Minami-ku, Kumamoto}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}; {{ill|Minami-ku|WD=Q876960}}| region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th-6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park, museum) | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|41|23|N|130|44|13|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Tsukawara Kofun Cluster'''|塚原古墳群|Tsukawara Kofun gun}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun| Kofun]]s, located in the Jonan neighborhood of {{ill|Minami-ku, Kumamoto|en|Minami-ku, Kumamoto|WD=Q876960|lt=Minami-ku}} in the city of [[:en:Kumamoto, Kumamoto|Kumamoto]], {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus cluster was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/164081|title=塚原古墳群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tsukawara Kofun cluster was discovered in 1972 during the construction of the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|en|Kyushu Expressway|WD=Q1060803|lt=Kyushu Expressway}}, and for preservation purposes, the route of the expressway was changed to a tunnel which passes directly underneath the site. The site was preserved as Tsukahara Kofun Park, and also contains the Kumamoto City Tsukahara History and Folklore Museum. This group of Kofuns is the largest in Kumamoto Prefecture and one of the largest in Japan, and was constructed in the 4th to 6th centuries. The park contains 39 {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} square moated tumuli, 34 {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} circular tumuli, and three {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}} keyhole-shaped tombs, with one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, along with 18 stone {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|WD=Q48634|lt=sarcophagi}}, and one stone-covered earthen mound; however, only the area immediately above the route of the Kyushu Expressway has been investigated and preserved. Of the two hills in the park, north and south, the large square moat tombs are mainly concentrated south of the northern hill, while the large circular tombs are concentrated around the northern hill and south of the southern hill. It is believed that there are many more such Kofuns outside the park, and in an exploratory survey of the entire Tsukahara Plateau in 1973, and additional 203 Kofuns were confirmed, and it was estimated that in the surrounding area there may be nearly 500 Kofuns in total. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
<gallery> Kumamoto Tsukawara Ancient Tomb Park 08.JPG| ''enpun''-style round kofun Kumamoto Tsukawara Ancient Tomb Park 04.JPG| ''hōfun''-style square kofun Kumamoto Tsukawara Ancient Tomb Park 01.JPG| Reconstructed building at Tsukawara Historical Museum Kumamoto City Tsukawara Historical Museum.JPG|Kumamoto City Tsukawara Historical Museum </gallery>
The site is about 30 minutes on car from {{ill|Uto Station|en|Uto Station|WD=Q874134|lt=Uto Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Kagoshima Main Line|en|Kagoshima Main Line|WD=Q1187520|lt=Kagoshima Main Line}}.
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)|WD=Q6573273|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:-inline|Tsukawara Ancient Tomb Park}} *[https://kumamoto-guide.jp/spots/detail/193 Kumamoto Heart-to-Heart Sightseeing guide]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://tsukawara.kumamoto-city-museum.jp|Kumamoto City Tsukahara History and Folklore Museum]{{in lang|ja}}

Of the two hills in the park, north and south, the large square moat tombs are mainly concentrated south of the northern hill, while the large circular tombs are concentrated around the northern hill and south of the southern hill. It is believed that there are many more such Kofuns outside the park, and in an exploratory survey of the entire Tsukahara Plateau in 1973, and additional 203 Kofuns were confirmed, and it was estimated that in the surrounding area there may be nearly 500 Kofuns in total. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

The site is about 30 minutes on car from {{ill|Uto Station|en|Uto Station|WD=Q874134|lt=Uto Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Kagoshima Main Line|en|Kagoshima Main Line|WD=Q1187520|lt=Kagoshima Main Line}}.

{{Short description|Kofun period keyhole-shaped Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tsukawara Kofun Cluster | native_name =塚原古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image =Kumamoto Tsukawara Ancient Tomb Park 07.JPG|image= File:Kumamoto Tsukawara Ancient Tomb Park 07.JPG| image_size = | alt = | caption = ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' at Tsukawara Kofun Cluster | map_type = Japan Kumamoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tsukawara Kofun Clsuter | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|32|41|23|N|130|44|13|E|display=inline, title}}; {{Coord|32.689722222222215|N|130.73694444444442|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location ={{ill|Minami-ku, Kumamoto|en|Minami-ku, Kumamoto|WD=Q876960|lt=Minami-ku, Kumamoto|1=Minami-ku, Kumamoto}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}; {{ill|Minami-ku|WD=Q876960|1=Minami-ku, Kumamoto}}| region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th-6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park, museum) | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580|1=Historic Site of Japan}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|41|23|N|130|44|13|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Tsukawara Kofun Cluster'''|塚原古墳群|Tsukawara Kofun gun}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun| Kofun]]s, located in the Jonan neighborhood of {{ill|Minami-ku, Kumamoto|en|Minami-ku, Kumamoto|WD=Q876960|lt=Minami-ku|1=Minami-ku, Kumamoto}} in the city of [[:en:Kumamoto, Kumamoto|Kumamoto]], {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus cluster was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/164081|title=塚原古墳群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tsukawara Kofun cluster was discovered in 1972 during the construction of the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|en|Kyushu Expressway|WD=Q1060803|lt=Kyushu Expressway|1=Kyushu Expressway}}, and for preservation purposes, the route of the expressway was changed to a tunnel which passes directly underneath the site. The site was preserved as Tsukahara Kofun Park, and also contains the Kumamoto City Tsukahara History and Folklore Museum. This group of Kofuns is the largest in Kumamoto Prefecture and one of the largest in Japan, and was constructed in the 4th to 6th centuries. The park contains 39 {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} square moated tumuli, 34 {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} circular tumuli, and three {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}} keyhole-shaped tombs, with one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, along with 18 stone {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|WD=Q48634|lt=sarcophagi|1=Sarcophagus}}, and one stone-covered earthen mound; however, only the area immediately above the route of the Kyushu Expressway has been investigated and preserved. Of the two hills in the park, north and south, the large square moat tombs are mainly concentrated south of the northern hill, while the large circular tombs are concentrated around the northern hill and south of the southern hill. It is believed that there are many more such Kofuns outside the park, and in an exploratory survey of the entire Tsukahara Plateau in 1973, and additional 203 Kofuns were confirmed, and it was estimated that in the surrounding area there may be nearly 500 Kofuns in total. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
<gallery> Kumamoto Tsukawara Ancient Tomb Park 08.JPG| ''enpun''-style round kofun Kumamoto Tsukawara Ancient Tomb Park 04.JPG| ''hōfun''-style square kofun Kumamoto Tsukawara Ancient Tomb Park 01.JPG| Reconstructed building at Tsukawara Historical Museum Kumamoto City Tsukawara Historical Museum.JPG|Kumamoto City Tsukawara Historical Museum </gallery>
The site is about 30 minutes on car from {{ill|Uto Station|en|Uto Station|WD=Q874134|lt=Uto Station|1=Uto Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Kagoshima Main Line|en|Kagoshima Main Line|WD=Q1187520|lt=Kagoshima Main Line|1=Kagoshima Main Line}}.
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)|WD=Q6573273|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]]{{template-inline|Tsukawara Ancient Tomb Park}} *[https://kumamoto-guide.jp/spots/detail/193 Kumamoto Heart-to-Heart Sightseeing guide]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://tsukawara.kumamoto-city-museum.jp|Kumamoto City Tsukahara History and Folklore Museum]{{in lang|ja}}

Of the two hills in the park, north and south, the large square moat tombs are mainly concentrated south of the northern hill, while the large circular tombs are concentrated around the northern hill and south of the southern hill. It is believed that there are many more such Kofuns outside the park, and in an exploratory survey of the entire Tsukahara Plateau in 1973, and additional 203 Kofuns were confirmed, and it was estimated that in the surrounding area there may be nearly 500 Kofuns in total. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

The site is about 30 minutes on car from {{ill|Uto Station|en|Uto Station|WD=Q874134|lt=Uto Station|1=Uto Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Kagoshima Main Line|en|Kagoshima Main Line|WD=Q1187520|lt=Kagoshima Main Line|1=Kagoshima Main Line}}.

{{nihongo|'''Tsukawara Kofun Cluster'''|塚原古墳群|Tsukawara Kofun gun}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun| Kofun]]s, located in the Jonan neighborhood of {{ill|Minami-ku, Kumamoto|en|Minami-ku, Kumamoto|WD=Q876960|lt=Minami-ku|1=Minami-ku, Kumamoto}} in the city of [[:en:Kumamoto, Kumamoto|Kumamoto]], {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus cluster was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/164081|title=塚原古墳群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tsukawara Kofun cluster was discovered in 1972 during the construction of the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|en|Kyushu Expressway|WD=Q1060803|lt=Kyushu Expressway|1=Kyushu Expressway}}, and for preservation purposes, the route of the expressway was changed to a tunnel which passes directly underneath the site. The site was preserved as Tsukahara Kofun Park, and also contains the Kumamoto City Tsukahara History and Folklore Museum. This group of Kofuns is the largest in Kumamoto Prefecture and one of the largest in Japan, and was constructed in the 4th to 6th centuries. The park contains 39 {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} square moated tumuli, 34 {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} circular tumuli, and three {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}} keyhole-shaped tombs, with one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, along with 18 stone {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|WD=Q48634|lt=sarcophagi|1=Sarcophagus}}, and one stone-covered earthen mound; however, only the area immediately above the route of the Kyushu Expressway has been investigated and preserved. Of the two hills in the park, north and south, the large square moat tombs are mainly concentrated south of the northern hill, while the large circular tombs are concentrated around the northern hill and south of the southern hill. It is believed that there are many more such Kofuns outside the park, and in an exploratory survey of the entire Tsukahara Plateau in 1973, and additional 203 Kofuns were confirmed, and it was estimated that in the surrounding area there may be nearly 500 Kofuns in total. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

{{Short description|Kofun period keyhole-shaped Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tsukawara Kofun Cluster | native_name =塚原古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image =Kumamoto Tsukawara Ancient Tomb Park 07.JPG|image= File:Kumamoto Tsukawara Ancient Tomb Park 07.JPG| image_size = | alt = | caption = ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' at Tsukawara Kofun Cluster | map_type = Japan Kumamoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tsukawara Kofun Clsuter | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|32|41|23|N|130|44|13|E|display=inline, title}}; {{Coord|32.689722222222215|N|130.73694444444442|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location ={{ill|Minami-ku, Kumamoto|en|Minami-ku, Kumamoto|WD=Q876960|lt=Minami-ku, Kumamoto|1=Minami-ku, Kumamoto}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}; {{ill|Minami-ku|WD=Q876960|1=Minami-ku, Kumamoto}}| region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th-6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park, museum) | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580|1=Historic Site of Japan}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|41|23|N|130|44|13|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Tsukawara Kofun Cluster'''|塚原古墳群|Tsukawara Kofun gun}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun| Kofun]]s, located in the Jonan neighborhood of {{ill|Minami-ku, Kumamoto|en|Minami-ku, Kumamoto|WD=Q876960|lt=Minami-ku|1=Minami-ku, Kumamoto}} in the city of [[:en:Kumamoto, Kumamoto|Kumamoto]], {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus cluster was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/164081|title=塚原古墳群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tsukawara Kofun cluster was discovered in 1972 during the construction of the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|en|Kyushu Expressway|WD=Q1060803|lt=Kyushu Expressway|1=Kyushu Expressway}}, and for preservation purposes, the route of the expressway was changed to a tunnel which passes directly underneath the site. The site was preserved as Tsukahara Kofun Park, and also contains the Kumamoto City Tsukahara History and Folklore Museum. This group of Kofuns is the largest in Kumamoto Prefecture and one of the largest in Japan, and was constructed in the 4th to 6th centuries. The park contains 39 {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} square moated tumuli, 34 {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} circular tumuli, and three {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun|1=[[Zenpō-kōen-fun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}} keyhole-shaped tombs, with one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, along with 18 stone {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|WD=Q48634|lt=sarcophagi|1=Sarcophagus}}, and one stone-covered earthen mound; however, only the area immediately above the route of the Kyushu Expressway has been investigated and preserved. Of the two hills in the park, north and south, the large square moat tombs are mainly concentrated south of the northern hill, while the large circular tombs are concentrated around the northern hill and south of the southern hill. It is believed that there are many more such Kofuns outside the park, and in an exploratory survey of the entire Tsukahara Plateau in 1973, and additional 203 Kofuns were confirmed, and it was estimated that in the surrounding area there may be nearly 500 Kofuns in total. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
<gallery> Kumamoto Tsukawara Ancient Tomb Park 08.JPG| ''enpun''-style round kofun Kumamoto Tsukawara Ancient Tomb Park 04.JPG| ''hōfun''-style square kofun Kumamoto Tsukawara Ancient Tomb Park 01.JPG| Reconstructed building at Tsukawara Historical Museum Kumamoto City Tsukawara Historical Museum.JPG|Kumamoto City Tsukawara Historical Museum </gallery>
The site is about 30 minutes on car from {{ill|Uto Station|en|Uto Station|WD=Q874134|lt=Uto Station|1=Uto Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Kagoshima Main Line|en|Kagoshima Main Line|WD=Q1187520|lt=Kagoshima Main Line|1=Kagoshima Main Line}}.
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)|WD=Q6573273|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)}}
